TextENGNew: The phone rang suddenly.
I bolted upright in a panic.
...The clock read nine p.m.
``...Damn it...!!@k@vS05/13/101300251. I overslept through the scheduled check-in...``
I picked up the receiver.
"Akasaka-sama.@k@vS05/00/front00002. You have a call from an outside line. Shall I put it through?@k@vS05/00/front00003. The caller says he is your colleague, Kanou-sama."
...Just as I'd feared.@k...Since I'd missed my scheduled check-in, they had called me instead.
``...I'm sorry, please connect them.``
``Understood.@k@vS05/00/front00005. I'll transfer the call, so please hang up and wait a moment.``
I hung up, and after a few seconds, the phone rang again.
``Yes, hello?@k@vS05/13/101300254. This is Akasaka...!``

"Akasaka-kun?@k@vS05/00/VV_kano_10015. This is Kanou.@k@vS05/00/VV_kano_10016. Good work out there.@k@vS05/00/VV_kano_10017. You didn't call in for your scheduled check-in, so I called you.@k@vS05/00/VV_kano_10018. How are things on your end?"
...Putting the scolding for my late report aside for now, my senior got straight to business.

"Today I went to Hinamizawa village for a reconnaissance visit.@k@vS05/13/101300256. Disguised as a tourist, I managed to make contact with the village chief and several other key members of the Defense Alliance.@k@vS05/13/101300257. I wasn't able to extract any information yet, but I left a good impression and laid the groundwork for tomorrow and beyond."

"I see.@k@vS05/00/VV_kano_10020. That's quite efficient of you.@k@vS05/00/VV_kano_10021. Your somewhat fresh-faced demeanor may have actually worked in your favor, Akasaka-kun.@k@vS05/00/VV_kano_10022. Sounds like a solid start?"
``...I don't have enough experience to know if it went well or not,@k@vS05/13/101300259. ...but at least today's reconnaissance went smoothly enough.``

``How does what you've found compare with the information from the prefectural police and the local public security division?@k@vS05/00/VV_kano_10024. Does it look like the Onigafuchi Defense Alliance is in the clear?``
changeReason: Org name changed from 'Guardians' to 'Defense Alliance'; question rephrased but meaning preserved.

TextENGNew: To that, I...
I still didn't know.
...I should have been able to give that answer immediately. All I had to do was say those words, and yet... for some reason, I hesitated.
``...I'm still in the middle of my investigation, so I can't say one way or another.``
I gave my senior my honest impressions.
``No, I don't know yet.@k@vS05/13/VX_akasaka_0012. I'll give it more time, but honestly, I'm not too hopeful about finding a connection.``
"I see.@k@vS05/00/VV_kano_10026. Try to be as quick as you can.@k@vS05/00/VV_kano_10027. You understand, I'm sure, but once tonight is over, it will have been a full four days since the kidnapping."
``...There's no hard deadline, but with every passing day, time works against us.@k@vS05/00/VV_kano_10028. The longer we take, the worse our position becomes.``
``Yes.@k@vS05/13/101300262. I understand.@k@vS05/13/101300263. I'll investigate as quickly and thoroughly as I can.``

"All right.@k@vS05/00/VV_kano_10031. Be careful out there.@k@vS05/00/VV_kano_10032. You're dealing with a violent organization.@k@vS05/00/VV_kano_10033. ...Let your guard down and there could be real danger.@k@vS05/00/VV_kano_10034. If anything seems dangerous, contact us immediately.@k@vS05/00/VV_kano_10035. We can arrange for the local Public Safety branch to provide backup."
``Thank you.@k@vS05/13/101300265. I'll do my best.``
``...Anything else to report on your end?``
I added a few supplementary details and administrative matters... and then, right at the very end, I asked.
``...How is the investigation going on your end?``
``Hmm.@k@vS05/00/VV_kano_10049. Everyone here is working hard, but we're not making much headway.@k@vS05/00/VV_kano_10050. It's frustrating.``
``So if you determine that the group you're investigating is clean, come back to Tokyo right away and help the others.``
``...The section chief wants us to look into Onigafuchi too, but personally, I think a local anti-dam residents' group like that has absolutely nothing to do with this case.``
``...Sending a man out on assignment just to investigate one group is a real strain on us, honestly.``
At this point, he was just venting.
...He was essentially telling me to stop enjoying my little business trip and hurry back.
``I'll try to wrap things up as soon as possible.``
``Good. I'm counting on you!@k@vS05/00/VV_kano_10055. Well, if there's nothing else, I'll hang up.``
``All right.@k@vS05/13/101300269. That's everything.``
Before hanging up, he made sure to give me a proper scolding for being late with my report.
After that, I set down the receiver and sprawled out on the bed.
...Come to think of it, I hadn't heard from Ooishi.
He was supposed to contact me tonight with the time and place for meeting the informant.
...I thought about venting my frustration from being scolded by taking it out on Ooishi, but...
On reflection, all Ooishi had said was that he would call sometime tonight.
...Waiting for a phone call when you had no idea when it would come was its own kind of torment.
Just as that thought crossed my mind, the phone rang. I snatched up the receiver.
``H-Hello!``

``Well, that's a surprise.@k@vS05/11/100700214. ...You pick up fast, Akasaka-san.@k@vS05/11/100700215. With reflexes like those, you could have a bright career ahead of you even after you quit the force.``
``I've never even considered quitting the force...!``
``The way you overreact is rather... entertaining.@k@vS05/11/100700217. Na ha ha ha!@k@vS05/11/100700218. Well then, shall we get down to business?``
``...I'll pass on the banter, so please get to the point, Ooishi-san.``
``Really?@k@vS05/11/100700221. That's a shame. We'll save the retirement talk for next time, then.@k@vS05/11/100700222. Now, let me tell you about the meeting place.``
Many of the Onigafuchi Defense Alliance's senior members wielded considerable influence in the surrounding area.
changeReason: Organization name changed from 'Onigafuchi Guardians' to 'Onigafuchi Defense Alliance' (死守同盟 means 'defend to the death alliance').

TextENGNew: Even in Okinomiya, there were plenty of people with ties to the village.
...That's why meeting here in the distant Shishibone entertainment district was the obvious choice.
``Akasaka-san, over here!@k@vS05/11/100700224. Get a bit lost?@k@vS05/11/100700225. It can be tricky to find if you're not from around here.``
``No, I'm sorry for being late.``
``Have you eaten dinner already?``
``Yes.@k@vS05/13/101300275. I've already eaten.``

"Well then, shall we go somewhere we can get a drink?@k@vS05/11/100700228. Western liquor?@k@vS05/11/100700229. Or sake?@k@vS05/11/100700230. And do you prefer a place with hostesses or without?@k@vS05/11/100700231. Mwu fu fu fu!"
``Don't worry about me.@k@vS05/13/101300277. I don't drink while on duty.``
Hearing that, the man Ooishi had brought along threw his head back in laughter, slapping Ooishi on the shoulder.
``See?@k@vS05/11/100700233. Isn't he adorable?@k@vS05/11/100700234. In exchange, I brought you this fresh-faced young man. So let's hear what you've got.``
The man Ooishi had brought looked like a ticket scalper or a bookie -- obviously shady from head to toe.
The informant broke into a mischievous grin.
``Hey, kid.@k@vS05/00/104400002. How much did you slip this guy?@k@vS05/00/104400003. Must've been quite a sum for him to bring you to me.``
``Ooishi's handling fee was included, so that's already accounted for.``
The informant had apparently expected a more colorful reaction from me.
...When I responded calmly, he seemed to recognize I was a completely different type of person than himself.
But rather than writing me off, he seemed intrigued, like someone encountering a rare specimen...

``I told you, didn't I?@k@vS05/11/100700237. He's the serious type.@k@vS05/11/100700238. But you know, Akasaka-san?@k@vS05/11/100700239. In a line of work like ours, a wealth of real-world experience is a tremendous asset.``
changeReason: Minor rewording: 'wealth of experience being a weapon' -> 'tremendous asset'. Same meaning.

TextENGNew: ``I'm wondering exactly how experience drinking at hostess bars constitutes a useful weapon...``
``Bwa ha ha ha ha ha ha!!``
The informant, unable to contain himself any longer, clutched his stomach and roared with laughter in full view of everyone.
``Oh my!@k@vS05/11/100700241. Akasaka-san, you're sharp!@k@vS05/11/100700242. Well, the informant's business isn't going to be done here on the street, so let's go.``
``...Ooishi-san?@k@vS05/13/101300281. You've been drinking somewhere already, haven't you!@k@vS05/13/101300282. I can tell!``
``What's wrong with drinking when I'm off duty~!@k@vS05/11/100700244. Even if you're on the clock, I'm off.@k@vS05/11/100700245. Na ha ha ha!``
...Ooishi, who struck me as the mischievous type to begin with, was in such high spirits that he was practically unmanageable.
...He was nothing like me, who had arrived wound tight with tension, determined not to miss a single word of the informant's precious intelligence.
...Ooishi-san.
You grabbed all that money with both hands, so you'd better deliver information worth every yen...
But then I realized that since it was the informant providing the information, it didn't matter how drunk the middleman, Ooishi, happened to be.
...I couldn't exactly complain, but it still irked me somehow.
Led by Ooishi and the informant (he went by Sato -- undoubtedly a false name), we wound deeper and deeper into the back alleys of the entertainment district.
Just a few streets off the bustling main strip and it became this desolate -- the nightlife district was full of surprises.
Ooishi started climbing a set of stairs on the side of a seedy-looking building.
There was no signage whatsoever. I couldn't even tell whether it was a business, let alone whether it was open.
``...Don't worry.@k@vS05/11/100700247. It's not a sketchy place.@k@vS05/11/100700248. It's perfectly respectable.``
No words from Ooishi's mouth could have been less convincing...
Beyond the door... was, thankfully, a perfectly ordinary mahjong parlor.
...I was relieved it wasn't the kind of dubious establishment I'd been bracing for...
``Welcome, welcome~.@k@vS05/00/tenin00002. Oh, Ooishi-san, long time no see!@k@vS05/00/tenin00003. I'll get a table ready right away.``
``No, no, I'm here with a group tonight, so that's fine.@k@vS05/11/100700250. Ah, sorry to keep you waiting.``
Mahjong requires four players.@k...Apparently, another member of the group had been waiting here already -- a middle-aged man rose from his seat.
``You're late, Kuraudo.@k@vS05/00/104800002. I was about to head home!``
Ooishi used me as an excuse for being late.
...Before long, the man noticed I was a new face and took an interest.
``This is Akasaka-san.@k@vS05/11/100700252. A promising rookie who came all the way from Tokyo on assignment.``
``...Kid.@k@vS05/00/104400006. Can you play mahjong?``
``W-Well... I played a fair bit... back in my student days.``
Sato and the old man cackled in unison.@k...Their plan to fleece me was painfully obvious...
``I figured you must be tired from being on edge during your assignment!@k@vS05/11/100700254. Why not unwind with a game tonight?@k@vS05/11/100700255. You do know how to play, don't you?``
``...I know this is a stupid question at this point, but... we're all police, aren't we...?``
``````Gwa ha ha ha ha ha ha!!``````
``Well~, let's not worry about that.@k@vS05/11/100700258. Relax, we won't actually be gambling.@k@vS05/11/100700259. The losers just have to take the winners out somewhere fun afterward.``
``...Somewhere fun?@k@vS05/13/101300286. What kind of place are you talking about?``
The three of them proceeded to rattle off the names of establishments so obviously dubious there was no mistaking what they were.
...At the very least, covering four people at any of those places would be no trivial expense.
``...Ooishi-san cleaned me out the other day, so payment is impossible, and I have work obligations, so I can't stay long...``
``Then just win!@k@vS05/00/104800005. If you win, you don't have to pay for the parlor, and you don't have to treat anyone to nightlife!``
...No matter how this played out, they clearly had no intention of footing the bill themselves...
...It seemed I would need to steel my resolve.
If I let them keep looking down on me, it would start affecting my work.
``...Understood.@k@vS05/13/101300289. I'll play with you for a little while.@k@vS05/13/101300290. But just for a bit. I'm on a tight schedule.``
``````Gwa ha ha ha ha ha!!``````
``Yeah, yeah!!@k@vS05/11/100700263. Bring it on!@k@vS05/11/100700264. Mfu fu fu!``
``If I win, we're heading straight to the Blue Mermaid on Flower Road!!``
``The old man sure loves that place~!!@k@vS05/11/100700266. I, of course, plan to make the rounds of all my favorite spots!@k@vS05/11/100700267. Mfu fu fu!``
``...I'd prefer a nice, 'quiet' place to drink.``

"A quiet place with a bunny girl, right?@k@vS05/11/100700271. Mwa ha ha ha ha ha!!@k@vS05/11/100700272. Last time we went drinking, you pulled the tail right off that bunny girl, didn't you?@k@vS05/11/100700273. Did you end up having to pay for that?"
``The owner started calling it a 'tailoring fee' and cracked open a bottle of Dom Perignon on my tab.``
````Wa ha ha ha ha ha!!````
...What a rowdy table.
...I could tell the other customers were glaring at us as our vulgar banter grew louder and louder...
...Worse yet, they probably assumed I was one of this lot... that was the depressing part...
...Ooishi had taken all my expense funds, so every yen I had left was out of my own pocket.@k...And it wasn't much.
...If I kept going along with them, they'd have me buying round after round until my wallet vanished into the night.
...I had to somehow win and steer the conversation back to work.
...But these guys... no matter how you looked at them, they were good...@k...Sorry, Yukie.
``Na ha ha ha!@k@|@y@vS05/11/100700276. Well then, I'll just declare riichi here!``
...Well, with such a transparent riichi, neither the old man nor Sato-san would fall for it.@kI'm hoping Akasaka-kun might slip up and throw out the winning tile, though!
``Kuraudo's riichi is probably going for something around this range, right?@k@vS05/00/104800009. This should be safe to throw.``
``There!``
Oh-ho!@kThe old man's not bad at all.@kHe quietly built his hand using one of my safe tiles, didn't he?
It seems Akasaka-kun has at least learned enough to avoid obvious traps.@k...But that's precisely what might be his undoing.
``...What's the matter, kid?@k@vS05/00/104800012. Hurry up and discard.@k@vS05/00/104800013. You're holding up the game.``
Ooh, Akasaka-kun's really agonizing over this one~!
It was obvious he had a safe tile to discard, but didn't want to throw it because it would push his hand further from tenpai.@kHeh, rookies are so much fun to watch!
Come on, just throw out the winning tile already~!
If he feeds it to me, we're off to a lovely establishment where mfu fu fu!
And if he feeds it to the old man, we're headed straight to the maid cafe with the bloomers~~!!
``...This one... will it go through?``
Looking up nervously, the tile he hesitantly discarded was...@k@|@y ...Oh well, too bad.
``Hey now, that's a dangerous tile, you know?!@k@vS05/00/104800015. Kuraudo, does that pass?!``
``Yes indeed, it passes~!@k@vS05/11/100700278. Mfu fu fu!``
Seems you got lucky this time around.
But the round's not over yet -- there are still plenty of draws to go!!@kNfu fu fu!
After that, Akasaka-kun somehow kept dodging and weaving with a mix of skill and luck, and the round ended in a draw.
``Tenpai.``
``Tenpai.``
``Noten.``
Sato-san had shrewdly anticipated the double riichi and opted to fold entirely.
Now that's a sharp, cool-headed approach.
Now then, let's see what Akasaka-kun's hand looked like.
In mahjong, when a round ends in a draw, anyone in tenpai has to reveal their hand.
But if you're not in tenpai, you're under no obligation to show.

...Your average beginner would normally show their hand even without tenpai and make a fuss about what went well or badly, or that they would have been in tenpai if this or that tile had come...@kBut he didn't do that.@k...Playing it cool?
``How'd it go, kid?@k@vS05/00/104400014. Let me take a look!``
Sato-san reached over to flip the hand Akasaka-kun had quietly placed face-down.
``Hmm~~...??@k@vS05/00/104400016. What the hell is this...?``
He was nowhere near tenpai.@k...Was Akasaka-kun completely hopeless after all?@kMfu fu fu...?
Just then, the old man started comparing Akasaka-kun's hand to his discards, and a puzzled look crossed his face.
``...Kid.@k@vS05/00/104800018. You were in tenpai right around here, weren't you?@k@vS05/00/104800019. Why did you tear apart your own hand?``

"...The higher man tiles suddenly shot up in value, so I held onto them.@k@vS05/13/101300294. Even though Ooishi-san's riichi had actually made them safer to discard.@k@vS05/13/101300295. It would have been routine to play them using suji logic, and yet someone deliberately held them back."
``...Oh?@k@vS05/00/104800021. ...So you dismissed Kuraudo's tenpai as a non-factor from the very start?``
``...Unless it was a truly sophisticated trap, I assumed it was a weak hand banking on me dealing in.@k@vS05/13/101300297. I decided it was more important to watch your hand instead.``
``When did you figure out I was in tenpai?``
``...Around here?@k@vS05/13/101300299. You discarded four tiles in rapid succession without hesitation.@k@vS05/13/101300300. That's when I started watching your hand more closely.``
...Akasaka-kun... you...
``Akasaka-san, how long have you been playing mahjong?``

"I only played from high school through university.@k@vS05/13/101300302. Now, let's continue.@k@vS05/13/101300303. Since Sato-san didn't reach tenpai, the hand ended in a draw—so it's my turn as dealer next, right?@k@vS05/13/101300304. ...Dealer."
...Something about Akasaka-kun's demeanor had... subtly shifted...
Into our cozy little setup to fleece him... a cold, focused tension had crept in...
``...Ooishi-san, you're being stingy with the bamboo tiles.@k@vS05/13/101300306. Going to cycle them?``
``...Tch.@k@vS05/00/104800024. ...This kid...!@k@vS05/00/104800025. Kuraudo, don't underestimate him!``
``If I can just draw three more good tiles...!@k@|@y@vS05/11/100700282. Sato-san, how's your hand looking?``
``Damn...!@k@vS05/00/104400018. I'll just drag him down with a cheap tanyao!@k@|@y@vS05/00/104400019. Chi!``
``A chi?@k@|@y@vS05/13/101300308. Sorry, but I'm calling pon on that.``
...Wait, he waited until after the chi was declared to call pon?!@k ...Which means... a blocking pon?!
``...Kid, you sure know your way around a table...!``
``Hardly.@k@vS05/13/101300310. ...I can't compare to people who've been playing since before I was born.@k@vS05/13/101300311. My hand, please.``
``Triplets, two red dora, three more dora.``
``...And I'm the dealer, so this is going to sting a little.``
``Na ha ha ha ha ha!!@k@vS05/11/100700284. Akasaka-san, well played!!@k@vS05/11/100700285. That was impressive!``
``Since we're being serious now, how about putting your points where your mouth is?@k@vS05/13/101300315. ...I'm tired, so let's finish this quickly, shall we?``
...Akasaka-kun?
...There's something wonderfully menacing about him...@kNahahahaha...
Before I knew it, there was an almost palpable cold aura emanating from beneath Akasaka-kun's feet...
``...Sloppy.@k@|@y@vS05/13/101300317. Don't you think the 2, 5, and 8 of man are laughing at you?``
``...Kuraudo, just deal into me!@k@|@y@vS05/00/104800029. We need to end the kid's dealer turn, now!``
``...I hate to admit it, but... that does seem like the wisest course of action...``
``Sorry -- head bump.@k@|@y@vS05/13/101300319. ...Ron.@k@|@y@vS05/13/101300320. Tanyao, three-color straight, red dora.``
``...A head bump?!@k@vS05/00/104400026. You little brat...!!``

"Brat?@k@vS05/13/101300322. You'd play sloppily because your opponent is a brat, is that it?@k@vS05/13/101300323. ...Then you're worse than a brat yourself.@k@vS05/13/101300324. ...That's rich.@k@vS05/13/101300325. You guys always say the same thing—calling me a brat, a kid.@k@vS05/13/101300326. Heh heh heh...!"
``A-Akasaka-kun?@k@vS05/11/100700289. That is you, isn't it?@k@vS05/11/100700290. ...You didn't get replaced by someone else, did you?``
``...You were planning to fleece me and drink the night away on my tab, weren't you?@k@vS05/13/101300328. But it seems you all need to tighten your game a bit more.``
``...Tanyao, pinfu, three-color straight, one red dora, one dora.``
``...Whoa!!@k@vS05/00/104400028. He actually pulled it off!!``
``That's what I'm saying -- you're all playing too loosely.@k@vS05/13/101300332. ...Concealed hand or not, with a wide-open three-sided wait like that, you should have been more careful.``
Akasaka-kun chuckled, chin resting in his hand.
...The way he carried himself was so effortlessly commanding, it was almost frightening.
``...Hey, Kuraudo!@k@vS05/00/104800031. This isn't what you promised!@k@vS05/00/104800032. You said he was fresh meat!``
``Well... na ha ha ha...!!@k@vS05/11/100700292. Now, who could have said that?``
``...Ooishi-san, this is bad.@k@vS05/00/104400030. If this keeps up, we're going to lose for real!``
...Akasaka-kun.@kYou're actually making me take this seriously.
...So you really want to be thoroughly demolished, is that it...
``Pon.@k@vS05/13/101300334. ...Pon.@k@vS05/13/101300335. ...How generous of you all.@k@vS05/13/101300336. I'll take them gladly.``
Akasaka-kun was calling tiles left and right with practiced ease.
...Every set he'd revealed was pin tiles.
...No way, is he serious... going for a full flush with that kind of brute-force approach?!
``...Kid... are you sane...?@k@vS05/00/104400032. With four sets open... you've only got one tile left in your hand.``
``That's right.@k@vS05/13/101300338. ...Did you know?@k@vS05/13/101300339. Waiting on a single tile is actually the hardest to read.``
``A-Are you an idiot?!@k@|@y@vS05/00/104400034. With that much of your hand exposed, who's going to deal into you now!?@k@vS05/00/104400035. Just fold already!``
``...Nfu fu fu!@k@vS05/11/100700294. ...I see right through you, Akasaka-san.``
``...That's not actually a full flush or a half flush, is it?``
``With four pons, you've already got triplets locked in.@k@vS05/11/100700297. ...What you're really doing is using your unassailable lead to play for a single-tile wait.``
``...If you really think so, want to try throwing out a pin tile?``
``The five tiles on the left side of your hand, Ooishi-san.@k@vS05/13/101300343. Those are pin tiles you've been failing to discard.@k@vS05/13/101300344. Go ahead. Throw one.``
...You've got to be kidding me...
Is this guy the god of mahjong...?@kIf not, he must be the protagonist of some gambling manga!!
Just then, the old man sent me a signal from under the table.@kThe message was simple and clear.
(...Kuraudo. ...Do it. Crush him.)
(...I don't like using tricks against a kid this young.)
(Fool! After being shown up like this, you think we can just back down?!)
(...Well, don't blame me for what happens. Go ahead.)
``Here we go, kid!@k@|@y@vS05/00/104800036. Riichi!!@k@vS05/00/104800037. Let's settle this once and for all!``
Most likely, the tile Akasaka-kun was waiting on was among those exposed tiles.
...With this, Akasaka-kun had lost his chance to win off a discard.
...When I drew from the wall... I swapped out the tile Akasaka-kun would draw next.
...The tile I slipped in was the one-pin.
One of the old man's winning tiles.
Akasaka-kun had already discarded it earlier, so there was no risk of it being his...
``...It's your turn, kid.``
I could see he was thrown off by drawing the one-pin.@k...Naturally, he couldn't discard it.
``Kid.@k@vS05/00/104800040. You know the rules, right? ...If you deal into an open riichi, it's a yakuman penalty.``
``You don't have to tell me that.``
The tile that came from Akasaka-kun's hand was...@k@|@y ...an honor tile!
Whew!! Now that's a gutsy move!
That North wind -- there's only one left in the wall, isn't there?@kYou were waiting on a single-tile tanki with that of all tiles?!
But... with this, Akasaka-kun's fate was sealed.
``...Heh heh, brat.``
``...You dodged that one.@k@vS05/00/104800043. ...But you've only got one tile left in your hand.@k@vS05/00/104800044. Next draw, you won't be so lucky.``
``...So that's how it is.``
Akasaka-kun fixed the old man with a razor-sharp glare.
...This guy had keen instincts.@kHad he figured out we were stacking the tiles?!
(...Let's stop this, old man. Akasaka-kun is two or three levels above us.)
(Don't be stupid!! We've come this far -- we can't stop now! Next one! Make him draw it!!)
...The next tile I'd make Akasaka-kun draw was the seven-pin.
...With that, it would be over.@kWhichever tile he discarded, he'd deal into the old man.
...An inescapable... yakuman payout!!
On my draw... I sneakily reached for Akasaka-san's next tile to swap it out---
``Achoo!!!``
*Crash!*
...What just happened?!
...Akasaka-kun suddenly sneezed and... knocked over the wall of tiles in front of him?!
``H-Hey now, what are you doing...``
``Sorry, I accidentally knocked over the wall when I sneezed.@k@vS05/13/101300351. ...Penalty payment, right?@k@vS05/13/101300352. I'll gladly pay it.``
``...Wa ha ha ha ha ha ha ha!!!``
I couldn't hold it in any longer. I burst out laughing.
Akasaka-kun, having been set up twice and knowing he was about to be cornered with an inescapable tile,@k...pulled off the audacious move of collapsing the wall himself to escape!
``...Not bad, kid...!!@k@vS05/00/104800048. To dodge a yakuman payout with a stunt like that!!@k@vS05/00/104800049. I'll remember this!``
``...Ooishi-san, could you please refrain from such obvious tile stacking?@k@vS05/13/101300355. ...In a real parlor, what you just pulled would get you thrown out and beaten.``
``Oh no no no no, sorry, sorry!@k@vS05/11/100700303. I completely underestimated you!@k@vS05/11/100700304. Na ha ha ha ha!``
``Answer me honestly.@k@vS05/00/104800051. You played quite a bit in your younger days, didn't you?``
``...I played around Waseda and Takadanobaba quite a bit.@k@vS05/13/101300357. I was winning so much that the stakes kept escalating... until I was playing for about twenty times the normal amount.``
``The stakes kept escalating?@k@vS05/00/104400037. How much were you playing for at your peak?``
``It depended on the group, but when I played in Kabukicho, I had an almost perfect record.@k@vS05/13/101300360. Especially against cheaters -- I never once lost to someone using tricks.``
Twenty times the normal stakes?!?!
What kind of games were those?!@kWere your opponents yakuza?@kOr foreign mafia?!
``...What's all this!?@k@vS05/00/104800053. Kuraudo!!@k@vS05/00/104800054. You buffoon, you set me up against a monster like this and let me embarrass myself!!``
``Thank you, it was an honor.``

"...Na ha ha ha...@k@vS05/11/100700306. Oh my.@k@vS05/11/100700307. ...Akasaka-san, you really change when you're at the table~!@k@vS05/11/100700308. Why did you even get a regular job!@k@vS05/11/100700309. You could easily make a living as a pro!"
``To be a professional mahjong player, you need both skill and luck.@k@vS05/13/101300363. I lacked the latter, so I never pursued it.``
``...Luck is just the breaks you get.@k@vS05/00/104400039. With skill like yours, I'd think you could make it work.``

``Normal luck isn't enough.@k@vS05/13/101300365. You'd need to call kan and have the dora indicator flip in your favor maybe one in three times.@k@vS05/13/101300366. At that level, I just can't compete.``
``...Akasaka-kun, if you could do that consistently, you'd be a psychic, not a mahjong player...``
``No, it's neither cheating nor psychic powers. There are quite a few players at that level.``

``There was a player I met in Shinjuku who would somehow draw dora every time he called a tile -- he was monstrously strong.@k@vS05/13/101300369. And another who could tell what tiles were coming just by the sound of the shuffle.``
...What kind of people were those...? Did they sell their souls to the devil or something...?
``So then, I believe the overall winner is me. Agreed?``
``Yes, yes~!@k@vS05/11/100700312. Total and utter defeat!@k@vS05/11/100700313. Mfu fu fu... but it was fun!``
``I lost, I lost!@k@vS05/00/104800061. Haven't been beaten this badly in ages~~!!``
``Still, kid... when you were in the zone, you were something else.@k@vS05/00/104400041. Honestly, it was kind of scary...``
``People tell me that a lot.@k@vS05/13/101300373. ...I don't think I change that much, personally.@k@vS05/13/101300374. But I do hear it often.``
The three of us nodded vigorously in agreement.
...So that was Akasaka-kun -- the mahjong prodigy.
...Before he heads back to Tokyo, maybe I could get him to sit in as a ringer at some high-stakes game!
...But no, I shouldn't get greedy.@kNfu fu!

"...Well, nothing for it.@k@vS05/11/100700316. A promise is a promise.@k@vS05/11/100700317. Let's call it a night for our little evening study session.@k@vS05/11/100700318. Sorry about this, old man.@k@vS05/11/100700319. We're done for tonight."
``Can't be helped.@k@vS05/00/104800064. I'm going home to bed!@k@vS05/00/104800065. Gotta save my energy for another grueling day at work!``
``Work giving you trouble?``
``Oh, the old man here is the foreman at the main construction site for the Hinamizawa Dam.@k@vS05/11/100700321. Daily skirmishes with the residents and all that.``
``...Next year, I might end up in charge of the second site too.@k@vS05/00/104800067. Kuroda-san's blood pressure is through the roof.@k@vS05/00/104800068. He might not last.``
``You'll be fine, old man.@k@vS05/11/100700324. You've got thicker skin than you look.@k@vS05/11/100700325. Na ha ha ha!``

``Even I have my limits, you know!@k@vS05/00/104800070. There are times my blood boils over!@k@vS05/00/104800071. That bratty daughter of Sonozaki's especially — I can't stand her!@k@vS05/00/104800072. One of these days, I swear I'll rearrange that smug face of hers!``
changeReason: Minor rewording; both preserve the Sonozaki daughter reference.

TextENGNew: ``Now, now, now!@k@vS05/11/100700327. But the pay is good, right?@k@vS05/11/100700328. You can put up with a lot for good pay.``
``Damn right it is!@k@vS05/00/104800074. If the pay were any worse, nobody would stick around in that hellhole!@k@vS05/00/104800075. But even good pay has its limits!``
``...Hmm... Well, who knows how things will turn out.``
The old man, who'd introduced himself as the dam's site foreman, grumbled for a while longer, but after getting it all off his chest, he seemed to feel better and headed home.
``Well then, let's go.@k@vS05/11/100700331. ...I'll leave the rest to Sato-san.``
``...Ooishi-san, you're heading out?``
``I'd just be in the way if I stuck around, right?@k@vS05/11/100700333. Besides, you're the one who paid Sato-san for his services.@k@vS05/11/100700334. I should let you get your money's worth.``
Rough around the edges, yet oddly principled where you'd least expect it.
...There was something of a generational gap between Ooishi and me in that regard.
``Well then, kid.@k@vS05/00/104400044. ...No, I can't call a mahjong master 'kid' anymore.@k@vS05/00/104400045. ...What should I call you?``
``Whatever you like.``
``Then, Akasaka-kun.@k@vS05/00/104400047. Shall we?``
``Well then, Akasaka-san.@k@vS05/11/100700336. Until next time.@k@vS05/11/100700337. ...If anything comes up, don't hesitate to call.``
``I don't have any money left to pay you with, you know.``
``How about you go easy on me next game and we'll call it even?@k@vS05/11/100700009. Na ha ha ha!``
Ooishi, who clearly still hadn't had his fill of entertainment, refrained from pushing any further and disappeared into the nighttime streets...
